title: A Weekend Under the Stars
summary: A narrator describes a camping weekend with friends — the fun of setting up the tent, cooking a simple meal outdoors, and drifting off to sleep beneath the open sky.
body: Last month, my friends and I went on a camping trip to a forest about two hours from the city. We drove there on Friday evening and arrived just before dark. Setting up the tent took longer than we expected because none of us had done it before. We tried to follow the instructions, but two of the poles kept falling down, and we ended up laughing more than working. Eventually, we got the tent up and felt very proud of ourselves. The next morning, we cooked eggs and toast over a small camping stove. Everything tasted better outdoors — even plain eggs felt like a proper breakfast. After eating, we went for a long walk through the trees and found a stream where we sat for a while and just listened to the water. In the afternoon, we played card games and told stories. When the sun went down, we lit a campfire and roasted marshmallows on sticks. That evening, we lay on our sleeping bags and looked up at the sky. There were thousands of stars — more than I had ever seen before. I felt very small, but in a good way. We fell asleep to the sound of the wind in the trees. It was one of the best weekends of my life.

annotations.exampleEn: We went on a camping trip by the lake last summer.
We reached the campsite just before dark.
Setting up the tent is easier with two people.
We got lost and ended up at the wrong station.
A camping stove is useful when there is no electricity.

annotations.meaningVi: đơn giản, không có thêm gia vị hay thứ gì khác
annotations.exampleEn: He prefers plain rice without any sauce.
We sat around the campfire and sang songs.
Roasting marshmallows over a fire is a classic camping tradition.
She lay on the grass and looked at the clouds.
He fell asleep on the sofa while watching TV.
I love falling asleep to the sound of rain.

annotations.meaningVi: cảm thấy thật nhỏ bé (so với thiên nhiên, vũ trụ)
annotations.exampleEn: Looking at the mountains, she felt very small.

grammarNotes.title: Simple past for completed narrative events
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi kể về một chuỗi sự kiện đã xảy ra và kết thúc trong quá khứ, ta dùng thì quá khứ đơn (simple past). Ví dụ: 'We drove there… arrived just before dark… set up the tent.' Mỗi động từ diễn tả một bước trong câu chuyện.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use simple past tense to narrate a sequence of completed actions in the past. Example: 'We drove there on Friday evening and arrived just before dark.' Each verb marks a new step in the story. Contrast with past continuous, which describes the background: 'the wind was blowing'.

grammarNotes.title: Comparatives: more than + past perfect
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'More than I had ever seen before' là cấu trúc so sánh kết hợp với thì quá khứ hoàn thành (had + past participle). Dùng past perfect để so sánh với những gì đã xảy ra trước đó trong cuộc đời. Ví dụ: 'There were more stars than I had ever seen before.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'More than I had ever seen before' combines a comparative with the past perfect (had + past participle). Use the past perfect to compare a past experience with everything before it. Example: 'It was the best weekend I had ever had.' This is a common pattern in personal storytelling.

translationVi: Tháng trước, bạn bè tôi và tôi đi cắm trại ở một khu rừng cách thành phố khoảng hai tiếng lái xe. Chúng tôi lái xe đến đó vào tối thứ Sáu và đến nơi ngay trước khi trời tối. Việc dựng lều mất nhiều thời gian hơn chúng tôi dự kiến vì không ai trong chúng tôi đã làm việc này trước đây. Chúng tôi cố gắng làm theo hướng dẫn, nhưng hai thanh cọc cứ đổ xuống, và cuối cùng chúng tôi cười nhiều hơn là làm việc. Cuối cùng, chúng tôi cũng dựng được lều và cảm thấy rất tự hào. Sáng hôm sau, chúng tôi nấu trứng và bánh mì nướng trên bếp dã ngoại nhỏ. Mọi thứ ăn ngoài trời đều ngon hơn — ngay cả trứng bình thường cũng cảm giác như một bữa sáng thực sự. Sau khi ăn, chúng tôi đi bộ dài trong rừng và tìm thấy một con suối, nơi chúng tôi ngồi một lúc và chỉ lắng nghe tiếng nước chảy. Buổi chiều, chúng tôi chơi bài và kể chuyện. Khi mặt trời lặn, chúng tôi đốt lửa trại và nướng kẹo dẻo trên những chiếc que. Tối hôm đó, chúng tôi nằm trên túi ngủ và nhìn lên bầu trời. Có hàng nghìn ngôi sao — nhiều hơn tôi chưa từng thấy trước đây. Tôi cảm thấy thật nhỏ bé, nhưng theo một nghĩa tốt. Chúng tôi chìm vào giấc ngủ khi nghe tiếng gió thổi qua những tán cây. Đó là một trong những cuối tuần tuyệt vời nhất của cuộc đời tôi.

questions.prompt: Where did the narrator and friends go camping?
options: By a lake near the city
On a beach two hours away
In a forest about two hours from the city
In the mountains three hours away
questions.explanation: They went to 'a forest about two hours from the city.'
questions.hint: Two pieces of information are given — the location type and the distance.

questions.prompt: Why did setting up the tent take longer than expected?
options: None of them had done it before
questions.explanation: It 'took longer than we expected because none of us had done it before.'
questions.hint: It was the group's first time doing this.

questions.prompt: What problem did they have while setting up the tent?
options: The tent had a hole in it
questions.explanation: 'Two of the poles kept falling down.'
questions.hint: A structural part of the tent kept failing.

questions.prompt: What did they cook the next morning?
questions.explanation: 'We cooked eggs and toast over a small camping stove.'
questions.hint: Two food items are mentioned — both breakfast classics.

questions.prompt: What did the narrator notice about eating outdoors?
options: Even simple food tasted better outside
It was hard to cook without electricity
questions.explanation: 'Everything tasted better outdoors — even plain eggs felt like a proper breakfast.'
questions.hint: The narrator makes a positive observation about outdoor eating.

questions.prompt: What did they find during their walk in the forest?
questions.explanation: 'We found a stream where we sat for a while and just listened to the water.'
questions.hint: It was a body of flowing water — smaller than a river.

questions.prompt: What did they do at the stream?
options: Just sat and listened to the water
questions.explanation: They 'sat for a while and just listened to the water.'
questions.hint: It was a quiet, restful activity.

questions.prompt: What did they do in the afternoon?
options: Played card games and told stories
questions.explanation: 'We played card games and told stories.'

questions.prompt: What did they do around the campfire in the evening?
questions.explanation: 'We lit a campfire and roasted marshmallows on sticks.'
questions.hint: Think of a classic camping treat cooked on a stick.

questions.prompt: What did the narrator notice when looking at the night sky?
options: He could see a distant city
questions.explanation: 'There were thousands of stars — more than I had ever seen before.'
questions.hint: The narrator had never seen so many before.

questions.prompt: What sound did they fall asleep to?
questions.explanation: 'We fell asleep to the sound of the wind in the trees.'
questions.hint: It was a natural sound from the forest.

cloze.template: Last month, my friends and I went on a camping {{b1}} to a forest about two hours from the city. We drove there on Friday evening and arrived just before dark. Setting up the tent took longer than we expected because none of us had done it before. We tried to follow the instructions, but two of the poles kept falling down, and we ended up {{b2}} more than working. Eventually, we got the tent up and felt very {{b3}} of ourselves. The next morning, we cooked eggs and toast over a small camping {{b4}}. Everything tasted better outdoors — even plain eggs felt like a proper breakfast. After eating, we went for a long walk through the trees and found a {{b5}} where we sat for a while and just listened to the water. In the afternoon, we played {{b6}} games and told stories. When the sun went down, we lit a {{b7}} and roasted marshmallows on sticks. That evening, we lay on our sleeping bags and looked up at the sky. There were thousands of {{b8}} — more than I had ever seen before. I felt very {{b9}}, but in a good way. We fell {{b10}} to the sound of the wind in the trees. It was one of the best {{b11}} of my life.

blanks.explanation: 'trip' — a camping trip is the standard phrase for a short camping excursion.
'laughing' — they ended up laughing more than working while struggling with the tent.
'proud' — they felt proud of themselves for successfully setting up the tent.
'stove' — a camping stove is a portable cooker used outdoors.
'stream' — a small, narrow flowing body of water found in a forest.
'card' — they played card games in the afternoon.
'campfire' — a controlled outdoor fire used for warmth, cooking, and atmosphere at a campsite.
'stars' — they saw thousands of stars, more than ever before.
'small' — 'felt very small' is used to describe the feeling of being insignificant compared to nature.
'weekends' — 'one of the best weekends of my life' is the narrator's final evaluation.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The narrator says he 'felt very small, but in a good way' when looking at the stars. Can feeling small sometimes be a positive experience? What kinds of things — in nature or in everyday life — can make us feel humbled, and why might that be important?
